articles.headline: Nation Observes Good Governance Day, PM Highlights Citizen-Centric Reforms
articles.explainer:
• Celebrated annually on December 25th, marking the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
• Prime Minister's address emphasized the government's commitment to transparency, accountability, and citizen participation.
• Focus on leveraging digital technologies for efficient public service delivery and reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Key initiatives discussed included further strengthening of e-governance platforms and grievance redressal mechanisms.
prelimsPoints: Good Governance Day is observed on December 25th.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
The concept aims to promote accountability and transparency in government.
mainsPoints: Significance of Good Governance Day in promoting ethical governance.
Challenges in achieving good governance in India and government initiatives to address them.
Role of technology (e-governance) in enhancing transparency and service delivery.
Impact of citizen participation on improving governance outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing the nation on Good Governance Day, with a portrait of Atal Bihari Vajpayee in the background.
articles.trivia: Atal Bihari Vajpayee served as Prime Minister of India for three terms, a total of over six years, and was known for his efforts in improving India's infrastructure and foreign relations.

articles.headline: Tributes Paid to Bharat Ratna Madan Mohan Malaviya on his 163rd Birth Anniversary
articles.explainer:
• Nation remembers Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, a towering figure in India's freedom struggle and education.
• Known for founding the Banaras Hindu University (BHU) in 1916, a landmark institution for modern education.
• Advocated for national unity, social reform, and the promotion of Indian culture and values.
• His contributions to journalism and the Indian National Congress were also highlighted.
prelimsPoints: Madan Mohan Malaviya's birth anniversary is on December 25th.
He founded the Banaras Hindu University (BHU).
He was conferred with the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 2014.
mainsPoints: Malaviya's multi-faceted contributions to education, nationalism, and social reform.
The vision behind establishing BHU and its impact on modern Indian education.
His role as a moderate leader in the Indian National Congress.
Relevance of his ideals of national integration and cultural preservation in contemporary India.
articles.imageDescription: A historical photograph of Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya.
articles.trivia: Madan Mohan Malaviya was also a prominent lawyer and served as the President of the Indian National Congress four times.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Green Hydrogen Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved a new PLI scheme aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and green hydrogen components.
• This initiative is a crucial step under India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting a global leadership position in green hydrogen production.
• The scheme will incentivize research and development, facilitate technology adoption, and reduce import dependence in the sector.
• Expected to create numerous jobs and contribute significantly to India's energy transition and climate goals.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers for green hydrogen.
It is part of the broader National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and decarbonization efforts.
Role of PLI schemes in fostering indigenous manufacturing and technological self-reliance.
Economic and environmental benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production.
Challenges in infrastructure development, cost reduction, and market creation for green hydrogen.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting a green hydrogen production facility with electrolysers and renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing fossil fuel imports worth over ₹1 lakh crore.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved crucial maneuvers including autonomous landing and precise navigation, simulating a return from space.
• This breakthrough is expected to dramatically reduce the cost of space access, making satellite launches more economical.
• The RLV program is vital for India's long-term space ambitions, including future human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV program aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The recent test focused on autonomous landing and navigation.
RLVs reduce the cost of space missions by allowing reuse of components.
mainsPoints: Significance of RLV technology for India's competitive edge in the global space market.
Impact of reduced launch costs on future satellite deployment and space exploration.
Challenges and technological advancements required for fully operational RLVs.
Contribution of RLV to India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan).
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) during re-entry into Earth's atmosphere.
articles.trivia: The concept of reusable launch vehicles dates back to the Space Shuttle program, but ISRO's RLV-TD is designed to be fully autonomous.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Critical Infrastructure Project with Stricter Safeguards
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the environmental clearance for a crucial infrastructure project.
• The ruling emphasized the delicate balance between economic development and environmental protection, mandating stricter safeguards.
• Directed the project proponents to adhere to the 'polluter pays' principle and ensure robust compensatory afforestation measures.
• The court also called for the establishment of independent monitoring mechanisms to oversee environmental compliance.
prelimsPoints: The 'polluter pays' principle holds polluters responsible for environmental damage.
Compensatory afforestation is planting trees to compensate for forest loss elsewhere.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is a mandatory process for projects.
mainsPoints: Role of the judiciary in balancing developmental goals with environmental conservation.
Challenges in ensuring effective implementation of environmental safeguards in large projects.
Critically analyze the 'polluter pays' principle and its effectiveness in India.
Discuss the need for robust monitoring and post-clearance compliance mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books, symbolizing a court decision, with a background image of a green forest and construction site.
articles.trivia: India's environmental laws draw heavily from international conventions and principles like sustainable development and precautionary principle.

articles.headline: India Launches 'Climate-Smart Agriculture' Initiative to Enhance Farmers' Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new national initiative focused on 'Climate-Smart Agriculture' (CSA).
• The program aims to equip farmers with sustainable practices to mitigate the adverse effects of climate change, such as droughts and floods.
• Key components include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and integrated pest management.
• It also emphasizes the use of technology for weather forecasting, soil health monitoring, and precision farming.
prelimsPoints: Climate-Smart Agriculture (CSA) focuses on increasing productivity, enhancing adaptation, and reducing emissions.
It includes practices like diversification, water harvesting, and use of climate-resilient seeds.
The initiative is crucial for India's food security in the face of climate change.
mainsPoints: Impact of climate change on Indian agriculture and the need for CSA.
Components and benefits of implementing CSA practices for small and marginal farmers.
Challenges in the widespread adoption of CSA in diverse agro-climatic zones.
Government's role in promoting sustainable agricultural practices and ensuring food security.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field, demonstrating climate-smart agriculture techniques like drip irrigation and intercropping.
articles.trivia: The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations developed the concept of Climate-Smart Agriculture.

articles.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Global South Cooperation at UN General Assembly
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN proposed a comprehensive framework to enhance cooperation among countries of the Global South.
• The framework focuses on collective action in areas such as climate change, equitable debt relief, and technology transfer.
• It advocates for greater representation of developing nations in global governance institutions to ensure a more inclusive world order.
• The proposal aims to strengthen South-South cooperation mechanisms and achieve sustainable development goals collectively.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies of developed and developing countries
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to developing countries, largely in the Southern Hemisphere.
South-South Cooperation involves collaboration among these countries.
India is a prominent voice for the Global South in international forums like the UN.
mainsPoints: India's leadership role and aspirations within the Global South.
Challenges faced by developing countries (e.g., climate change, debt, technology gap).
Significance of multilateralism and reforming global institutions for a more equitable world order.
How enhanced South-South cooperation can contribute to achieving SDGs.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of various developing nations arranged around a globe, symbolizing global south cooperation.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' gained prominence after the Cold War, highlighting geopolitical and economic disparities.

articles.headline: Ministry of Home Affairs Launches National Cyber Security Strategy 2024
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs unveiled the comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy 2024 to bolster India's digital defenses.
• The strategy focuses on protecting critical information infrastructure, fostering threat intelligence sharing, and enhancing incident response capabilities.
• It emphasizes the importance of public-private partnerships and international collaboration to combat sophisticated cyber threats.
• Aims to build a resilient and secure cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government operations.
gsSubTopics: Role of external state and non-state actors
prelimsPoints: The National Cyber Security Strategy aims to protect India's cyberspace.
It involves collaboration between government, private sector, and international partners.
CERT-In is India's national agency for cyber security incident response.
mainsPoints: Growing challenges of cyber security in the digital age, including state-sponsored attacks and cybercrime.
Key pillars of the National Cyber Security Strategy and its effectiveness in mitigating threats.
Importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in cyber defense.
Impact of cyber security on national security, economic stability, and individual privacy.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network, with locks and protective shields, symbolizing cyber security.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally, making robust cyber security essential.

articles.headline: Government Expands Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to Remote Tribal Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has been expanded to cover remote and underserved tribal regions across India.
• This expansion aims to provide digital health IDs, facilitate telemedicine consultations, and integrate health records for tribal communities.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the healthcare gap by improving accessibility and quality of medical services in challenging terrains.
• Efforts are also underway to integrate traditional medicine systems prevalent in tribal areas with modern healthcare practices.
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
Digital Health ID is a key component of ABDM.
Telemedicine facilitates remote medical consultations.
mainsPoints: Challenges faced by tribal populations in accessing quality healthcare services.
How digital health initiatives like ABDM can bridge these gaps.
Ethical considerations and data privacy concerns in digital health records.
Government's comprehensive approach to achieving universal health coverage, including vulnerable sections.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor consulting a tribal patient via telemedicine on a digital screen, symbolizing healthcare access in remote areas.
articles.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.

articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious National Logistics Policy 2024 to Boost Supply Chain Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The government launched the National Logistics Policy 2024, aiming to significantly reduce logistics costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
• The policy focuses on integrating various modes of transport—road, rail, air, and waterways—through a unified digital platform.
• Key objectives include promoting multimodal logistics parks, streamlining regulatory processes, and adopting advanced technologies like AI and IoT.
• Expected to boost India's manufacturing competitiveness, increase exports, and create new employment opportunities.
prelimsPoints: The National Logistics Policy aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
It promotes multimodal connectivity and integrated planning.
PM Gati Shakti is a complementary initiative for infrastructure development.
mainsPoints: Importance of a robust logistics sector for India's economic growth and global trade.
Challenges faced by India's logistics sector (e.g., high costs, infrastructure gaps, fragmentation).
How the new National Logistics Policy addresses these challenges through technology and integration.
Impact of improved logistics efficiency on manufacturing, exports, and 'Make in India' initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various modes of transport (truck, train, ship, plane) connected by digital lines, symbolizing integrated logistics.
articles.trivia: India's logistics cost is estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than the global average of 8-10%.

articles.headline: New Scheme Launched to Promote Organic Farming and Natural Farming Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new scheme to extensively promote organic and natural farming practices across the country.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, technical guidance, and certification support to farmers transitioning to sustainable agriculture.
• Emphasis is placed on improving soil health, enhancing biodiversity, and reducing reliance on chemical fertilizers and pesticides.
• It also aims to create market linkages for organic produce, ensuring better returns for farmers.
prelimsPoints: Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
Natural farming (Zero Budget Natural Farming) relies on natural processes and minimal external inputs.
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) is a scheme promoting organic farming.
mainsPoints: Benefits of organic and natural farming for environmental sustainability and farmer income.
Challenges in scaling up these practices, including certification, market access, and yield concerns.
Government's role in incentivizing and supporting farmers in adopting sustainable agriculture.
Contribution of these practices to food security and public health.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy, chemical-free crops in an organic farm, symbolizing sustainable agriculture.
articles.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Announces Grants for Preservation of Intangible Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has launched a new grant scheme to support the preservation and promotion of India's rich Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH).
• The grants will fund initiatives focused on documenting, revitalizing, and transmitting traditional arts, crafts, oral traditions, and performing arts.
• The scheme aims to empower local communities and cultural organizations in safeguarding endangered heritage forms.
• This move seeks to strengthen India's cultural identity and enhance its soft power on the global stage, aligning with UNESCO's conventions.
prelimsPoints: Intangible Cultural Heritage includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, and traditional craftsmanship.
UNESCO works to safeguard ICH globally.
India has several elements inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
mainsPoints: Importance of preserving ICH for national identity and cultural diversity.
Challenges in the documentation, transmission, and revitalization of traditional art forms.
Role of government and civil society in safeguarding ICH.
How cultural heritage contributes to India's soft power and cultural diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of diverse Indian traditional art forms, including dance, music, and crafts, representing intangible cultural heritage.
articles.trivia: The Kumbh Mela, Yoga, and Novruz are some of India's elements recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation highlighted significant progress in skill development initiatives.
• The review noted enhanced integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education.
• Emphasis was placed on industry-academia collaboration to align curricula with market demands and boost employability.
• The policy's focus on upskilling and reskilling programs is addressing critical skill gaps in the workforce, aligning with India's demographic dividend.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system.
It proposes integrating vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
Skill India Mission is a key government initiative for skill development.
mainsPoints: Key features of NEP 2020 related to skill development and vocational education.
Challenges in integrating vocational education into mainstream curricula and overcoming societal perceptions.
Importance of skill development for leveraging India's demographic dividend and boosting economic growth.
Role of industry-academia collaboration in making education more relevant to employment needs.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development and practical learning.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 system.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Early Warning Systems for Extreme Weather Events
articles.explainer:
• India's meteorological department announced significant upgrades to its early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• The enhancements involve integrating advanced satellite data, Artificial Intelligence (AI) models, and an expanded network of ground sensors.
• The aim is to improve the accuracy and timeliness of predictions for cyclones, floods, heatwaves, and other natural disasters.
• These systems are crucial for enhancing community preparedness and minimizing loss of life and property in disaster-prone regions.
prelimsPoints: IMD (India Meteorological Department) is responsible for weather forecasting.
Early Warning Systems (EWS) help in disaster preparedness.
AI and satellite data are used to improve prediction accuracy.
mainsPoints: Increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events due to climate change.
Importance of robust Early Warning Systems in disaster risk reduction.
Role of technology (AI, satellite imagery) in enhancing forecasting capabilities.
Challenges in last-mile connectivity and community response in disaster management.
articles.imageDescription: A satellite image showing a cyclone approaching the Indian coast, with weather radar overlays.
articles.trivia: India has significantly improved its cyclone early warning system since the 1999 Odisha super cyclone, leading to drastic reductions in fatalities.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase II Launched with Emphasis on Climate-Resilient Urban Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the launch of Smart Cities Mission Phase II, focusing on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
• The new phase prioritizes green buildings, efficient public transportation, and integrated waste management systems.
• It aims to promote sustainable urban planning, enhance citizen participation, and leverage technology for smart governance solutions.
• The mission seeks to address the challenges of rapid urbanization while ensuring environmental sustainability and livability in Indian cities.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure and a decent quality of life.
Phase II focuses on climate resilience and sustainability.
AMRUT and PMAY are other urban development schemes.
mainsPoints: Challenges posed by rapid urbanization in India and the need for smart urban solutions.
Key features and objectives of Smart Cities Mission Phase II, particularly its climate focus.
Role of technology and citizen participation in achieving smart and sustainable cities.
Critically evaluate the progress and impact of the Smart Cities Mission on urban governance and infrastructure.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of a green and technologically advanced smart city with sustainable buildings and public transport.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015, with 100 cities selected for development under its first phase.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Technical Discussions
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during ongoing technical discussions with Pakistan.
• The discussions, held under the aegis of the Permanent Indus Commission, focused on resolving differences over hydropower projects on western rivers.
• India emphasized its right to utilize waters for non-consumptive purposes, adhering strictly to treaty provisions.
• The treaty, brokered by the World Bank, remains a cornerstone of water-sharing arrangements between the two nations.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies of developed and developing countries
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960.
It allocates three Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and three Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan.
The World Bank brokered the treaty.
mainsPoints: Historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty for India-Pakistan relations.
Challenges and disputes that have arisen over the interpretation and implementation of the treaty.
India's strategy in utilizing its share of waters while adhering to treaty obligations.
Role of water diplomacy in managing transboundary river disputes.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers under the Indus Waters Treaty.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: GST Council Concludes Meeting, Focus on Simplifying Compliance for MSMEs
articles.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council concluded its latest meeting, with a primary agenda of simplifying compliance procedures for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
• Key decisions included potential rationalization of certain tax rates and easing of input tax credit rules for specific sectors.
• Discussions also revolved around leveraging technology to further streamline GST filing and reduce the burden on small businesses.
• The Council aims to boost economic activity and formalization within the MSME sector, which is a significant contributor to employment and GDP.
prelimsPoints: GST Council is the governing body for GST in India.
It is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
MSMEs are vital for India's economy and employment.
mainsPoints: Impact of GST on MSMEs and the challenges they face in compliance.
Role of the GST Council in tax policy decisions and its federal structure.
How simplification of GST can boost formalization and growth of the MSME sector.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of GST in achieving its original objectives of 'One Nation, One Tax'.
articles.imageDescription: A calculator displaying GST calculations, surrounded by charts and graphs, representing economic policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The GST Council's decisions require a three-fourths majority of the weighted votes of the members present and voting.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Defence Acquisition Policy 2024 Emphasizing Indigenization
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released the new Defence Acquisition Policy (DAP) 2024, focusing heavily on promoting indigenous manufacturing.
• The policy aims to streamline the procurement process, encourage private sector participation, and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.
• It introduces new categories for 'Make in India' and 'Buy Indian' to reduce reliance on foreign military imports.
• The goal is to achieve self-reliance in defense production, enhance export potential, and modernize India's armed forces with cutting-edge technology.
gsSubTopics: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
prelimsPoints: DAP governs the procurement of capital acquisitions for the Indian armed forces.
Indigenization aims to reduce dependence on foreign suppliers.
'Make in India' in defence promotes domestic manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of indigenization in the defence sector for national security and economic growth.
Key features of DAP 2024 and how it promotes domestic defence manufacturing.
Challenges faced by India in achieving self-reliance in defence production.
Role of private sector and MSMEs in strengthening India's defence industrial base.
articles.imageDescription: Modern Indian fighter jets and tanks, symbolizing indigenous defence capabilities, with the 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of military equipment, but recent policies aim to reverse this trend.

highlights: Nation celebrates Good Governance Day, marking Atal Bihari Vajpayee's birth anniversary with focus on citizen-centric reforms.
Tributes paid to Bharat Ratna Madan Mohan Malaviya, remembering his contributions to education and nationalism.
Union Cabinet approves new PLI scheme for Green Hydrogen manufacturing, boosting India's renewable energy goals.
ISRO successfully tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, paving way for cost-effective space access.
Supreme Court upholds environmental clearance for key projects, balancing development with stricter ecological safeguards.

prelimsMocks.question: When is Good Governance Day (Sushasan Divas) celebrated in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Good Governance Day is celebrated annually on December 25th to commemorate the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee. The day aims to promote awareness among people about government accountability and transparency.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following institutions was founded by Bharat Ratna Madan Mohan Malaviya, whose birth anniversary is also celebrated on December 25th?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, a prominent educationist and freedom fighter, founded the Banaras Hindu University (BHU) in 1916. He was a staunch advocate for national education and cultural revival.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Green Hydrogen manufacturing:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
2. The scheme primarily targets the import of electrolysers to meet domestic demand.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PLI scheme for Green Hydrogen manufacturing aims to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and components, thereby reducing import dependence, not targeting import itself. Its primary goal is to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary advantage of a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) in space technology, as being developed by ISRO?
options: (b) Significantly reduced launch costs per mission
(c) Faster launch turnaround time for heavy payloads
(d) Ability to deploy multiple satellites simultaneously
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Significantly reduced launch costs per mission
prelimsMocks.explanation: The main benefit of Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLVs) is the substantial reduction in the cost of accessing space. By allowing launch vehicle components to be reused, much like aircraft, the overall cost per launch can be drastically lowered, making space missions more economical.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is a key objective of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), which is being expanded to tribal areas?
options: (a) Providing free health insurance to all citizens below the poverty line.
(b) Establishing a network of primary health centers in every village.
(c) Creating digital health IDs for citizens and integrating their health records.
(d) Focusing solely on urban healthcare infrastructure development.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Creating digital health IDs for citizens and integrating their health records.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country. A core component is the creation of unique digital health IDs for citizens, linking their health records across various healthcare providers for seamless access and exchange of information.

mainsMocks.question: "Good Governance is a prerequisite for achieving sustainable development and ensuring citizen welfare." In light of this statement, discuss the key principles of good governance and evaluate India's progress in implementing them, particularly through initiatives celebrated on Good Governance Day.
mainsMocks.approach: Define good governance and its core principles (e.g., transparency, accountability, rule of law, participation, equity, efficiency). Link these principles to the vision of leaders like Atal Bihari Vajpayee. Discuss specific Indian initiatives (e.g., RTI, Citizen Charters, e-governance platforms, public service delivery mechanisms) that reflect these principles. Evaluate the successes and challenges in their implementation, providing concrete examples. Conclude with recommendations for further strengthening good governance in India.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission, supported by schemes like the new PLI for manufacturing, aims to position the country as a global hub for green hydrogen production. Analyze the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals. What are the key challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and what policy interventions are needed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Energy Sector; Environmental Conservation; Industrial Policy
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining what green hydrogen is and its production methods. Discuss its strategic importance for India: reducing fossil fuel imports (energy security), decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors, and meeting climate change commitments. Enumerate key challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development (storage, transport), water availability, technological maturity, and market demand creation. Suggest policy interventions, including R&D funding, regulatory frameworks, international collaborations, and financial incentives like PLI schemes, to overcome these challenges.

mainsMocks.question: Climate change poses a significant threat to agricultural productivity and food security in India. Discuss the concept of 'Climate-Smart Agriculture' (CSA) and its potential to enhance farmers' resilience. What are the practical challenges in adopting CSA practices across diverse agro-climatic zones in India, and how can these be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Agriculture; Environmental Degradation; Climate Change
mainsMocks.approach: Define Climate-Smart Agriculture (CSA) as an approach that sustainably increases productivity and incomes, adapts and builds resilience to climate change, and reduces greenhouse gas emissions. Explain how CSA practices (e.g., drought-resistant crops, water-efficient irrigation, diversified farming, agroforestry) enhance farmer resilience. Discuss practical challenges in adoption: lack of awareness, financial constraints for small farmers, fragmented landholdings, access to technology, inadequate extension services, and region-specific suitability. Conclude with policy recommendations and strategies to promote widespread CSA adoption.

quickBytes.text: Good Governance Day is celebrated on December 25th, the birth anniversary of former PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
Madan Mohan Malaviya, founder of Banaras Hindu University, also shares his birth anniversary on December 25th.
Green Hydrogen, produced by electrolysis using renewable energy, is crucial for India's decarbonization goals.
PLI (Production-Linked Incentive) schemes aim to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence in key sectors.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program seeks to achieve cost-effective and sustainable space access.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) creates digital health IDs for citizens to integrate their health records.
Climate-Smart Agriculture integrates productivity, adaptation, and mitigation to enhance farmers' resilience to climate change.
The National Logistics Policy aims to reduce logistics costs and improve supply chain efficiency through multimodal transport.
Intangible Cultural Heritage includes oral traditions, performing arts, rituals, and traditional craftsmanship.
India's new National Cyber Security Strategy focuses on protecting critical infrastructure and combating cybercrime.

GS1.headline: Tributes Paid to Bharat Ratna Madan Mohan Malaviya on his 163rd Birth Anniversary
GS1.explainer:
• Nation remembers Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, a towering figure in India's freedom struggle and education.
• Known for founding the Banaras Hindu University (BHU) in 1916, a landmark institution for modern education.
• Advocated for national unity, social reform, and the promotion of Indian culture and values.
• His contributions to journalism and the Indian National Congress were also highlighted.
prelimsPoints: Madan Mohan Malaviya's birth anniversary is on December 25th.
He founded the Banaras Hindu University (BHU).
He was conferred with the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 2014.
mainsPoints: Malaviya's multi-faceted contributions to education, nationalism, and social reform.
The vision behind establishing BHU and its impact on modern Indian education.
His role as a moderate leader in the Indian National Congress.
Relevance of his ideals of national integration and cultural preservation in contemporary India.
GS1.imageDescription: A historical photograph of Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya.
GS1.trivia: Madan Mohan Malaviya was also a prominent lawyer and served as the President of the Indian National Congress four times.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Announces Grants for Preservation of Intangible Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has launched a new grant scheme to support the preservation and promotion of India's rich Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH).
• The grants will fund initiatives focused on documenting, revitalizing, and transmitting traditional arts, crafts, oral traditions, and performing arts.
• The scheme aims to empower local communities and cultural organizations in safeguarding endangered heritage forms.
• This move seeks to strengthen India's cultural identity and enhance its soft power on the global stage, aligning with UNESCO's conventions.
prelimsPoints: Intangible Cultural Heritage includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, and traditional craftsmanship.
UNESCO works to safeguard ICH globally.
India has several elements inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
mainsPoints: Importance of preserving ICH for national identity and cultural diversity.
Challenges in the documentation, transmission, and revitalization of traditional art forms.
Role of government and civil society in safeguarding ICH.
How cultural heritage contributes to India's soft power and cultural diplomacy.
GS1.imageDescription: A collage of diverse Indian traditional art forms, including dance, music, and crafts, representing intangible cultural heritage.
GS1.trivia: The Kumbh Mela, Yoga, and Novruz are some of India's elements recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO.

GS2.headline: Nation Observes Good Governance Day, PM Highlights Citizen-Centric Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• Celebrated annually on December 25th, marking the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
• Prime Minister's address emphasized the government's commitment to transparency, accountability, and citizen participation.
• Focus on leveraging digital technologies for efficient public service delivery and reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Key initiatives discussed included further strengthening of e-governance platforms and grievance redressal mechanisms.
prelimsPoints: Good Governance Day is observed on December 25th.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee.
The concept aims to promote accountability and transparency in government.
mainsPoints: Significance of Good Governance Day in promoting ethical governance.
Challenges in achieving good governance in India and government initiatives to address them.
Role of technology (e-governance) in enhancing transparency and service delivery.
Impact of citizen participation on improving governance outcomes.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing the nation on Good Governance Day, with a portrait of Atal Bihari Vajpayee in the background.
GS2.trivia: Atal Bihari Vajpayee served as Prime Minister of India for three terms, a total of over six years, and was known for his efforts in improving India's infrastructure and foreign relations.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Critical Infrastructure Project with Stricter Safeguards
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the environmental clearance for a crucial infrastructure project.
• The ruling emphasized the delicate balance between economic development and environmental protection, mandating stricter safeguards.
• Directed the project proponents to adhere to the 'polluter pays' principle and ensure robust compensatory afforestation measures.
• The court also called for the establishment of independent monitoring mechanisms to oversee environmental compliance.
prelimsPoints: The 'polluter pays' principle holds polluters responsible for environmental damage.
Compensatory afforestation is planting trees to compensate for forest loss elsewhere.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is a mandatory process for projects.
mainsPoints: Role of the judiciary in balancing developmental goals with environmental conservation.
Challenges in ensuring effective implementation of environmental safeguards in large projects.
Critically analyze the 'polluter pays' principle and its effectiveness in India.
Discuss the need for robust monitoring and post-clearance compliance mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books, symbolizing a court decision, with a background image of a green forest and construction site.
GS2.trivia: India's environmental laws draw heavily from international conventions and principles like sustainable development and precautionary principle.

GS2.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Global South Cooperation at UN General Assembly
GS2.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN proposed a comprehensive framework to enhance cooperation among countries of the Global South.
• The framework focuses on collective action in areas such as climate change, equitable debt relief, and technology transfer.
• It advocates for greater representation of developing nations in global governance institutions to ensure a more inclusive world order.
• The proposal aims to strengthen South-South cooperation mechanisms and achieve sustainable development goals collectively.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies of developed and developing countries
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to developing countries, largely in the Southern Hemisphere.
South-South Cooperation involves collaboration among these countries.
India is a prominent voice for the Global South in international forums like the UN.
mainsPoints: India's leadership role and aspirations within the Global South.
Challenges faced by developing countries (e.g., climate change, debt, technology gap).
Significance of multilateralism and reforming global institutions for a more equitable world order.
How enhanced South-South cooperation can contribute to achieving SDGs.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of various developing nations arranged around a globe, symbolizing global south cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' gained prominence after the Cold War, highlighting geopolitical and economic disparities.

GS2.headline: Government Expands Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to Remote Tribal Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has been expanded to cover remote and underserved tribal regions across India.
• This expansion aims to provide digital health IDs, facilitate telemedicine consultations, and integrate health records for tribal communities.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the healthcare gap by improving accessibility and quality of medical services in challenging terrains.
• Efforts are also underway to integrate traditional medicine systems prevalent in tribal areas with modern healthcare practices.
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
Digital Health ID is a key component of ABDM.
Telemedicine facilitates remote medical consultations.
mainsPoints: Challenges faced by tribal populations in accessing quality healthcare services.
How digital health initiatives like ABDM can bridge these gaps.
Ethical considerations and data privacy concerns in digital health records.
Government's comprehensive approach to achieving universal health coverage, including vulnerable sections.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor consulting a tribal patient via telemedicine on a digital screen, symbolizing healthcare access in remote areas.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation highlighted significant progress in skill development initiatives.
• The review noted enhanced integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education.
• Emphasis was placed on industry-academia collaboration to align curricula with market demands and boost employability.
• The policy's focus on upskilling and reskilling programs is addressing critical skill gaps in the workforce, aligning with India's demographic dividend.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system.
It proposes integrating vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
Skill India Mission is a key government initiative for skill development.
mainsPoints: Key features of NEP 2020 related to skill development and vocational education.
Challenges in integrating vocational education into mainstream curricula and overcoming societal perceptions.
Importance of skill development for leveraging India's demographic dividend and boosting economic growth.
Role of industry-academia collaboration in making education more relevant to employment needs.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development and practical learning.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 system.

GS2.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase II Launched with Emphasis on Climate-Resilient Urban Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the launch of Smart Cities Mission Phase II, focusing on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
• The new phase prioritizes green buildings, efficient public transportation, and integrated waste management systems.
• It aims to promote sustainable urban planning, enhance citizen participation, and leverage technology for smart governance solutions.
• The mission seeks to address the challenges of rapid urbanization while ensuring environmental sustainability and livability in Indian cities.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure and a decent quality of life.
Phase II focuses on climate resilience and sustainability.
AMRUT and PMAY are other urban development schemes.
mainsPoints: Challenges posed by rapid urbanization in India and the need for smart urban solutions.
Key features and objectives of Smart Cities Mission Phase II, particularly its climate focus.
Role of technology and citizen participation in achieving smart and sustainable cities.
Critically evaluate the progress and impact of the Smart Cities Mission on urban governance and infrastructure.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of a green and technologically advanced smart city with sustainable buildings and public transport.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015, with 100 cities selected for development under its first phase.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Technical Discussions
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during ongoing technical discussions with Pakistan.
• The discussions, held under the aegis of the Permanent Indus Commission, focused on resolving differences over hydropower projects on western rivers.
• India emphasized its right to utilize waters for non-consumptive purposes, adhering strictly to treaty provisions.
• The treaty, brokered by the World Bank, remains a cornerstone of water-sharing arrangements between the two nations.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies of developed and developing countries
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960.
It allocates three Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and three Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan.
The World Bank brokered the treaty.
mainsPoints: Historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty for India-Pakistan relations.
Challenges and disputes that have arisen over the interpretation and implementation of the treaty.
India's strategy in utilizing its share of waters while adhering to treaty obligations.
Role of water diplomacy in managing transboundary river disputes.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers under the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Green Hydrogen Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved a new PLI scheme aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and green hydrogen components.
• This initiative is a crucial step under India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting a global leadership position in green hydrogen production.
• The scheme will incentivize research and development, facilitate technology adoption, and reduce import dependence in the sector.
• Expected to create numerous jobs and contribute significantly to India's energy transition and climate goals.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers for green hydrogen.
It is part of the broader National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and decarbonization efforts.
Role of PLI schemes in fostering indigenous manufacturing and technological self-reliance.
Economic and environmental benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production.
Challenges in infrastructure development, cost reduction, and market creation for green hydrogen.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting a green hydrogen production facility with electrolysers and renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing fossil fuel imports worth over ₹1 lakh crore.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved crucial maneuvers including autonomous landing and precise navigation, simulating a return from space.
• This breakthrough is expected to dramatically reduce the cost of space access, making satellite launches more economical.
• The RLV program is vital for India's long-term space ambitions, including future human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV program aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The recent test focused on autonomous landing and navigation.
RLVs reduce the cost of space missions by allowing reuse of components.
mainsPoints: Significance of RLV technology for India's competitive edge in the global space market.
Impact of reduced launch costs on future satellite deployment and space exploration.
Challenges and technological advancements required for fully operational RLVs.
Contribution of RLV to India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan).
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) during re-entry into Earth's atmosphere.
GS3.trivia: The concept of reusable launch vehicles dates back to the Space Shuttle program, but ISRO's RLV-TD is designed to be fully autonomous.

GS3.headline: India Launches 'Climate-Smart Agriculture' Initiative to Enhance Farmers' Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new national initiative focused on 'Climate-Smart Agriculture' (CSA).
• The program aims to equip farmers with sustainable practices to mitigate the adverse effects of climate change, such as droughts and floods.
• Key components include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and integrated pest management.
• It also emphasizes the use of technology for weather forecasting, soil health monitoring, and precision farming.
prelimsPoints: Climate-Smart Agriculture (CSA) focuses on increasing productivity, enhancing adaptation, and reducing emissions.
It includes practices like diversification, water harvesting, and use of climate-resilient seeds.
The initiative is crucial for India's food security in the face of climate change.
mainsPoints: Impact of climate change on Indian agriculture and the need for CSA.
Components and benefits of implementing CSA practices for small and marginal farmers.
Challenges in the widespread adoption of CSA in diverse agro-climatic zones.
Government's role in promoting sustainable agricultural practices and ensuring food security.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field, demonstrating climate-smart agriculture techniques like drip irrigation and intercropping.
GS3.trivia: The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations developed the concept of Climate-Smart Agriculture.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Home Affairs Launches National Cyber Security Strategy 2024
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs unveiled the comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy 2024 to bolster India's digital defenses.
• The strategy focuses on protecting critical information infrastructure, fostering threat intelligence sharing, and enhancing incident response capabilities.
• It emphasizes the importance of public-private partnerships and international collaboration to combat sophisticated cyber threats.
• Aims to build a resilient and secure cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government operations.
gsSubTopics: Role of external state and non-state actors
prelimsPoints: The National Cyber Security Strategy aims to protect India's cyberspace.
It involves collaboration between government, private sector, and international partners.
CERT-In is India's national agency for cyber security incident response.
mainsPoints: Growing challenges of cyber security in the digital age, including state-sponsored attacks and cybercrime.
Key pillars of the National Cyber Security Strategy and its effectiveness in mitigating threats.
Importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in cyber defense.
Impact of cyber security on national security, economic stability, and individual privacy.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network, with locks and protective shields, symbolizing cyber security.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally, making robust cyber security essential.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious National Logistics Policy 2024 to Boost Supply Chain Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The government launched the National Logistics Policy 2024, aiming to significantly reduce logistics costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
• The policy focuses on integrating various modes of transport—road, rail, air, and waterways—through a unified digital platform.
• Key objectives include promoting multimodal logistics parks, streamlining regulatory processes, and adopting advanced technologies like AI and IoT.
• Expected to boost India's manufacturing competitiveness, increase exports, and create new employment opportunities.
prelimsPoints: The National Logistics Policy aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
It promotes multimodal connectivity and integrated planning.
PM Gati Shakti is a complementary initiative for infrastructure development.
mainsPoints: Importance of a robust logistics sector for India's economic growth and global trade.
Challenges faced by India's logistics sector (e.g., high costs, infrastructure gaps, fragmentation).
How the new National Logistics Policy addresses these challenges through technology and integration.
Impact of improved logistics efficiency on manufacturing, exports, and 'Make in India' initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various modes of transport (truck, train, ship, plane) connected by digital lines, symbolizing integrated logistics.
GS3.trivia: India's logistics cost is estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than the global average of 8-10%.

GS3.headline: New Scheme Launched to Promote Organic Farming and Natural Farming Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new scheme to extensively promote organic and natural farming practices across the country.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, technical guidance, and certification support to farmers transitioning to sustainable agriculture.
• Emphasis is placed on improving soil health, enhancing biodiversity, and reducing reliance on chemical fertilizers and pesticides.
• It also aims to create market linkages for organic produce, ensuring better returns for farmers.
prelimsPoints: Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
Natural farming (Zero Budget Natural Farming) relies on natural processes and minimal external inputs.
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) is a scheme promoting organic farming.
mainsPoints: Benefits of organic and natural farming for environmental sustainability and farmer income.
Challenges in scaling up these practices, including certification, market access, and yield concerns.
Government's role in incentivizing and supporting farmers in adopting sustainable agriculture.
Contribution of these practices to food security and public health.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy, chemical-free crops in an organic farm, symbolizing sustainable agriculture.
GS3.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Early Warning Systems for Extreme Weather Events
GS3.explainer:
• India's meteorological department announced significant upgrades to its early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• The enhancements involve integrating advanced satellite data, Artificial Intelligence (AI) models, and an expanded network of ground sensors.
• The aim is to improve the accuracy and timeliness of predictions for cyclones, floods, heatwaves, and other natural disasters.
• These systems are crucial for enhancing community preparedness and minimizing loss of life and property in disaster-prone regions.
prelimsPoints: IMD (India Meteorological Department) is responsible for weather forecasting.
Early Warning Systems (EWS) help in disaster preparedness.
AI and satellite data are used to improve prediction accuracy.
mainsPoints: Increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events due to climate change.
Importance of robust Early Warning Systems in disaster risk reduction.
Role of technology (AI, satellite imagery) in enhancing forecasting capabilities.
Challenges in last-mile connectivity and community response in disaster management.
GS3.imageDescription: A satellite image showing a cyclone approaching the Indian coast, with weather radar overlays.
GS3.trivia: India has significantly improved its cyclone early warning system since the 1999 Odisha super cyclone, leading to drastic reductions in fatalities.

GS3.headline: GST Council Concludes Meeting, Focus on Simplifying Compliance for MSMEs
GS3.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council concluded its latest meeting, with a primary agenda of simplifying compliance procedures for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
• Key decisions included potential rationalization of certain tax rates and easing of input tax credit rules for specific sectors.
• Discussions also revolved around leveraging technology to further streamline GST filing and reduce the burden on small businesses.
• The Council aims to boost economic activity and formalization within the MSME sector, which is a significant contributor to employment and GDP.
prelimsPoints: GST Council is the governing body for GST in India.
It is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
MSMEs are vital for India's economy and employment.
mainsPoints: Impact of GST on MSMEs and the challenges they face in compliance.
Role of the GST Council in tax policy decisions and its federal structure.
How simplification of GST can boost formalization and growth of the MSME sector.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of GST in achieving its original objectives of 'One Nation, One Tax'.
GS3.imageDescription: A calculator displaying GST calculations, surrounded by charts and graphs, representing economic policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The GST Council's decisions require a three-fourths majority of the weighted votes of the members present and voting.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Defence Acquisition Policy 2024 Emphasizing Indigenization
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released the new Defence Acquisition Policy (DAP) 2024, focusing heavily on promoting indigenous manufacturing.
• The policy aims to streamline the procurement process, encourage private sector participation, and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.
• It introduces new categories for 'Make in India' and 'Buy Indian' to reduce reliance on foreign military imports.
• The goal is to achieve self-reliance in defense production, enhance export potential, and modernize India's armed forces with cutting-edge technology.
gsSubTopics: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
prelimsPoints: DAP governs the procurement of capital acquisitions for the Indian armed forces.
Indigenization aims to reduce dependence on foreign suppliers.
'Make in India' in defence promotes domestic manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Strategic importance of indigenization in the defence sector for national security and economic growth.
Key features of DAP 2024 and how it promotes domestic defence manufacturing.
Challenges faced by India in achieving self-reliance in defence production.
Role of private sector and MSMEs in strengthening India's defence industrial base.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern Indian fighter jets and tanks, symbolizing indigenous defence capabilities, with the 'Make in India' logo.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of military equipment, but recent policies aim to reverse this trend.

sources: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
